ideas.title: Choose Twitch stream lane
ideas.desc: Decide whether first Twitch streams run as OpSpot brand, Colton founder/build-in-public, or stay parked until lane is approved.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Choose first stream format
ideas.desc: Pick build-in-public, audit teardown, Q&A, workflow walkthrough, whiteboard, or clip-first mini stream.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Approve Twitch content pillars
ideas.desc: Proposed pillars: AI employees, dropped follow-up, CRM reality, document chase, owner bottlenecks, build receipts.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Approve Twitch draft cadence
ideas.desc: Proposal: draft stream plans locally; optional weekly/biweekly stream only after go-live/account/privacy policy approval.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Human click: Twitch login + OBS path
ideas.desc: Need Twitch account/login path and OBS availability/scene collection. No account connection, settings change, or go-live without approval.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Confirm OBS scene checklist
ideas.desc: Confirm scenes: starting soon, talking head, screen share, whiteboard, BRB/privacy pause, ending/CTA.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Confirm camera/mic + test recording
ideas.desc: Confirm camera, mic, audio levels, lighting/background, and local test recording path before any go-live card.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Approve privacy + permissions rules
ideas.desc: Set rules for customer proof, screenshots, private data, screen-share boundaries, notifications, and customer/logo/result permissions.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Approve chat moderation policy
ideas.desc: Choose chat rules, banned topics, mod owner, agent boundaries, escalation/kill switch. No public chat actions by agents without approval.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Money gate: Twitch tools/mods/editors stay off
ideas.desc: Approve/reject paid plugins, subscriptions, moderators, editors, ads/boosts. Default is $0 and local drafts only.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Approve clip + repurpose outputs
ideas.desc: Choose post-stream outputs: Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, recap, captions, thumbnails, and CTA review. No publishing without platform gates.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Explicit Twitch go-live approval
ideas.desc: Final hard gate: approve title/category/account, OBS scene, camera/mic, privacy checklist, moderation, and kill switch. No live stream without this.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Twitch HITL workflow pass — swipe-deck blocker/approval card.

ideas.title: Choose YouTube channel lane
ideas.desc: Decide whether first videos ship under OpSpot brand, Colton founder, client/project, or stay parked until channel direction is approved.
ideas.research: Decision gate before public posting or channel setup.

ideas.title: Approve YouTube brand + thumbnail style
ideas.desc: Choose handle/name/about/banner/avatar and thumbnail style direction before any public channel changes or final thumbnails.
ideas.research: Human taste gate; specialists can draft variants locally.

ideas.title: Approve first YouTube content pillars
ideas.desc: Proposed pillars: audit wedge, AI employees, CRM reality, dropped-work map, build-in-public receipts.
ideas.research: Locks what strategy/title/script specialists should optimize around.

ideas.title: Approve YouTube draft cadence
ideas.desc: Proposal: 1-2 Shorts packets/day or one long-form section/day; weekly package = 1 long-form + 3-5 Shorts. Upload cadence disabled until final policy approval.
ideas.research: Draft cadence only, no uploads.

ideas.title: Provide sample videos / proof inputs
ideas.desc: Need 1-3 raw clips, screen recordings, voice notes, demos, or customer-safe examples to turn scripts into real production packets.
ideas.research: Specialists continue automatically after samples are provided.

ideas.title: Confirm camera/mic setup
ideas.desc: Confirm recording setup and constraints: talking-head, screen recording, whiteboard, mic choice, and where raw files land.
ideas.research: Producer/recording specialist blocked until confirmed.

ideas.title: Approve proof + permissions policy
ideas.desc: Decide whether customer names/logos/results/private screenshots are forbidden, allowed by case, or require written permission per asset.
ideas.research: Default: no customer proof without explicit approval.

ideas.title: Human click: YouTube login/API/scheduler path
ideas.desc: Decide if/when to connect YouTube account, API, or scheduler. Default remains placeholder-only with no account changes.
ideas.research: External account/plugin connection gate.

ideas.title: Money gate: editors/tools/ads stay off
ideas.desc: Approve or reject any paid editor, thumbnail tool, subscription, API, ad/boost, or outsourcing spend. Default: $0.
ideas.research: No money movement without explicit approval.

ideas.title: Final metadata review gate
ideas.desc: Before upload, approve title, thumbnail, description, chapters, pinned comment, end screen/cards, schedule, and risk flags.
ideas.research: Final human review before any public mutation.

ideas.title: Explicit upload/schedule approval
ideas.desc: Final card for upload or schedule. Approve/revise/defer. No live upload without this explicit approval.
ideas.research: Hard stop gate for publishing specialist.

ideas.title: Approve draft cadence
ideas.desc: Choose cadence: draft 1-3/day; proposed public cadence 3 Reels/week + 1 carousel/week only after posting policy approval.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Approve customer-proof rules
ideas.desc: Set rule for customer names/logos/results. Default: no customer proof unless explicitly approved and sanitized.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Choose visual/voice direction
ideas.desc: Pick batch-1 style: clean operator cockpit, local trust, founder-led, or bold direct-response.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Confirm camera/mic setup + recording inputs
ideas.desc: Confirm camera/mic are ready and provide 1-3 raw inputs: clip, screenshot, voice note, demo, or customer pain.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Money gate: no boosts/tools by default
ideas.desc: Confirm no-spend policy, or approve a specific editing/scheduling tool budget. Default: $0, local drafts only.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Human click: Instagram login/tool path
ideas.desc: Need the account/login path and decision on scheduler/plugin. No account connection, profile mutation, or post until approved.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Approve first 3 Instagram pillars
ideas.desc: Approve/steer the first batch pillars: dropped follow-up, CRM truth, customer-document chase; or replace with better pains.
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Choose Instagram lane
ideas.desc: Decision: should first Instagram content publish from OpSpot brand, Colton founder, or stay parked until account strategy is cleaner?
ideas.research: 2026-05-08 Instagram HITL workflow pass — tomorrow morning swipe-deck blocker card.

ideas.title: Add monthly digest email — show MoM growth %
ideas.desc: Customer churn is high in months 2-3. A digest showing their numbers improving creates a "loss aversion" moment that retains 30%+ in similar SaaS plays.
ideas.research: Stripe blog · 2024 SaaS retention study

ideas.title: Inline error explainer — "what does this mean for me?"
ideas.desc: Power users skip past technical errors. A one-line plain-language translation under each error reduces support tickets ~20%.
ideas.research: Linear changelog · "humanized errors" pattern

ideas.title: Convoy mode: 3-agent parallel for big tasks
ideas.desc: Large features stall single agents. Parallel decomposition with DAG dependency would cut wall-clock 60%.
ideas.research: Karpathy AutoResearch · 2025 paper

ideas.title: Pricing page: stick the CTA on scroll
ideas.desc: Competitors all stick. Frankenclip's pricing CTA is below the fold on mobile. Easy lift, measurable.
ideas.research: Stripe / Linear / Vercel pricing pages

ideas.title: Auto-disqualify roofs <5yr old
ideas.desc: Solar pitches on new roofs waste 40% of outreach. Aging detection from satellite already in pipeline; just gate on it.
ideas.research: Internal — last 90d outreach data

draft.subject: Quick roofing follow-up audit idea
draft.body:
Hey there,

I was looking at Wilmington roofing contractor placeholder and noticed a likely OpSpot fit: roofing fit: missed calls, estimate booking, and slow follow-up are likely revenue leaks; website still needs verification.

The angle I would test is simple: AI receptionist + estimate follow-up for missed calls and after-hours leads.

If useful, I can show you how an AI employee would answer missed/after-hours calls, book estimates, and send follow-ups without adding another hire.

Worth a quick look?

— Colton

workflowMetadata.orchestratorRole: parent orchestrator routes each stage; does not become a 50-tool mega-agent
workflowMetadata.specialistAgent: research-agent-marley / lead-hunt specialist for sourcing; auditor specialist for scoring; lead-enrich specialist for contact data; outreach-compose specialist for drafts
workflowMetadata.approvalPolicy: Local research/audit/enrich only overnight. External sends, replies, calls, customer-visible changes, private/financial access, and purchases require explicit human approval; quiet hours freeze 8pm–8am ET.

workflowMetadata.workflow: Sales → Onboarding Flow v1 — lead reply to customer setup
workflowMetadata.orchestratorRole: Parent orchestrator routes each handoff, writes receipts, and keeps external actions disabled unless Colton/policy explicitly enables the run.
workflowMetadata.specialistAgent: reply handler → meeting-booker/notetaker → auditor → sales specialist → customer setup specialist → implementation specialist
requiredPlugins: message/email/iMessage send runners only after approval
workflowMetadata.approvalPolicy: Local prep/drafts/receipts are allowed. External sends/replies/calls, calendar writes, pricing/contracts, account/private/financial access, purchases, and customer-visible setup changes need approval unless an explicit policy-run is enabled.

agentActionCatalog.name: Google Maps lead puller
agentActionCatalog.summary: Pulls candidate local businesses from Maps/listing sources, then writes raw leads for audit. No outreach.
agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Draft locally only. External posts, account changes, DMs/comments, customer proof, boosts/spend, and autopost activation require explicit Colton approval.

agentActionCatalog.name: Morning / evening brief
agentActionCatalog.summary: Summarizes runs, output counts, cost/credit risks, blockers, and next 1–3 decisions for Colton.
agentActionCatalog.approvalPolicy: Local research, analysis, ranking, drafting, and queue generation may run alone. External sends/replies/calls, customer-visible changes, Apollo spend, private/financial access, purchases, or destructive actions require explicit Colton approval.

tellMeWhatYouWantBuilder.principle: rough intent → proposed workflow/policy → Colton steers/approves → specialist agents execute with receipts
tellMeWhatYouWantBuilder.example: “Find roofing leads around Wilmington and get me the best calls” becomes: source specialist → auditor → enrich gate → outbound queue → cold-call ranker → swipe deck approval.

replyHandler.guardrail: External replies/sends remain disabled until Colton explicitly enables approved run/policy.

salesOnboardingFlow.title: Tomorrow booked lead close/onboarding path
salesOnboardingFlow.primaryGoal: Close one first AI employee and complete onboarding on the spot.
salesOnboardingFlow.source: Digital Wave ads likely generated the booked meeting.
salesOnboardingFlow.approvalPolicy: Colton controls price, payment, customer-visible promises, sends, and account connections.

salesOnboardingFlow.input: Inbound reply + lead record + prior touch

salesOnboardingFlow.output: call prep card with opener, audit angle, recording/notes lane
salesOnboardingFlow.done: Colton can open one card and know who, why, what to ask, and what is blocked.

salesOnboardingFlow.input: Website/GBP/public footprint + vertical playbook + reply context
salesOnboardingFlow.output: proof, pain, ROI, recommended AI employee, risks/unknowns
salesOnboardingFlow.done: Packet is screen-share/proposal ready without pretending certainty.

salesOnboardingFlow.output: draft offer, scope, price placeholder, start path, approval gates
salesOnboardingFlow.done: Internal draft ready; customer-visible send still needs Colton approval.
Minimum setup inputs captured: owner, command channel, budget cap, memory/runtime lane, recording note.

salesOnboardingFlow.output: working AI employee + Business OS customer setup receipt
salesOnboardingFlow.done: Customer can message agent, receives first useful output, next integration is clear.

customerIntakeAgent.inspiration: invoice OCR style flow: file placeholder -> field extraction -> original/source link -> customer/lead update -> receipt
customerIntakeAgent.approvalPolicy: Local drafting/extraction only. No external reads, account connects, sends, or customer-visible mutations without explicit approval.

contentPipelines.afterApproval: generate scripts/captions → create post package → queue approval → publish only after explicit gate
script/metadata package → clip/shorts plan → upload draft or publish only after gate

contentPipelines.specialistAgent: Parent content orchestrator → Twitch positioning, run-of-show, OBS/production, moderation/safety, clip marker, repurpose, approval/publishing, compliance specialists
contentPipelines.afterApproval: parent proposes workflow/policy → Colton swipes decisions/clicks → positioning/run-of-show/OBS/moderation/clip/repurpose specialists continue automatically → go-live only after explicit final approval card

contextWallArchitecture.title: the most powerful AI tool.
contextWallArchitecture.principle: Organize company workflows/data/tools into folder/markdown skill packs so the parent orchestrator can route to narrow specialist agents without becoming a mega-agent.
missionControlNeeds: Tell Me What You Want builder
failure/retry lane independent of fragile chat sessions

contentAutomationV1.criticalArchitecturePrinciple: Parent orchestrator routes to Twitch/streaming/content specialists. No mega-agent with broad OBS/Twitch/moderation/publishing tools.

hormoziFrame.setup: Audience pain → live hook/transformation → stream meat/demo/proof → CTA → clip/repurpose loop; winners become future stream/short variations later.
hormoziFrame.ideas: Use customer pains, OpSpot queue, demos, build receipts, audits, and public-safe references; do not start with generic AI features.
hormoziFrame.liveHook: First 60 seconds names the workflow leak, promises the concrete outcome, and sets privacy boundaries.
hormoziFrame.rundown: 25–45 minute stream: cold open → problem → build/teach/demo → recap → CTA → clip marker review.
hormoziFrame.proof: Internal demos, mock examples labeled clearly, safe build receipts, and privacy-checked screen shares only; customer proof requires approval.
hormoziFrame.cta: audit / follow / checklist / book audit when path approved; DM/comment actions require separate approval.
hormoziFrame.clipsExtraction: Every approved stream plan includes intentional clip moments, timestamp sheet, post-stream clip candidates, captions, and CTA variants.
hormoziFrame.repurpose: Instagram Reels + YouTube Shorts + recap drafts are local-only until platform publish gates are approved.
hormoziFrame.cadence: Draft stream plans locally; optional weekly/biweekly stream cadence only after account, OBS, privacy, moderation, and go-live policy approval.
hormoziFrame.reviewGates: Morning swipe decisions/clicks/setup confirmations; final go-live card always explicit.

specialists.name: Parent content orchestrator
specialists.role: Turns rough stream intent into a Twitch workflow/policy proposal, routes bounded specialists, tracks approvals/receipts, and escalates true blockers.
tools: Tell Me What You Want builder

specialists.name: Stream positioning specialist
specialists.role: Defines audience, transformation, offer angle, stream promise, CTA, cadence, and format lane.

specialists.name: Run-of-show specialist
specialists.role: Creates title options, live hook, 3-5 segments, transitions, proof beats, chat prompts, and close/CTA.

specialists.name: OBS / production specialist
specialists.role: Owns scenes checklist, camera/mic checklist, screen-share privacy, local recording test, and go-live readiness checklist.

specialists.name: Chat moderation / safety specialist
specialists.role: Drafts chat policy, banned topics, response boundaries, escalation rules, and kill switch.

specialists.name: Clip marker specialist
specialists.role: Plans live clip moments, timestamp sheet, highlight labels, and post-stream extraction notes.

specialists.name: Instagram / YouTube repurpose specialist
specialists.role: Turns stream plan/recording into Reels, Shorts, captions, titles, thumbnails, and recap drafts.

specialists.name: Compliance / proof checker
specialists.role: Checks customer proof, private data, claims, permissions, unsupported metrics, and screen-share privacy.

intakeQuestions: What rough stream topic, business pain, demo, build, or reference are we starting from?
Which stream format: build-in-public, audit teardown, Q&A, workflow walkthrough, whiteboard, or clip-first mini stream?
Which lane: OpSpot brand, Colton founder, or parked?
What proof/receipt can we safely show on screen?
What CTA should this drive: audit, follow, checklist, book, or no hard CTA?

queueCards.label: Tell Me What You Want intake
Workflow + policy proposal
queueCards.output: specialists, tools, approvals, risks, next automation step

queueCards.label: Stream positioning packet
queueCards.output: audience, transformation, offer angle, CTA, cadence

queueCards.label: Run-of-show
queueCards.output: live hook, segments, talking points, proof beats, close/CTA

queueCards.label: OBS + scenes setup
queueCards.output: scenes, screen-share privacy, local recording, setup blockers

queueCards.label: Camera/mic confirmation
queueCards.output: device choices, audio levels, test recording, raw path

queueCards.label: Chat moderation + safety
queueCards.output: chat rules, banned topics, mod owner, kill switch

queueCards.label: Clip marker sheet
queueCards.output: intentional clip moments, timestamp sheet, extraction notes

queueCards.label: Instagram/YouTube repurpose
queueCards.output: Reels/Shorts candidates, captions, titles, CTA variants

queueCards.label: Receipts
queueCards.output: files changed, artifacts, blockers, next automation step

canDoAlone: draft positioning/rundown/checklists/moderation policy/clip markers/repurpose packets locally

contentAutomationV1.nextAutomationStep: After Colton approves/steers the Twitch swipe cards, parent orchestrator dispatches stream positioning → run-of-show → OBS/production → moderation/safety → clip marker → repurpose specialists automatically until an account, setup, privacy, money, chat, proof, or go-live gate blocks.
blockers: OBS scenes and local recording path not confirmed
escalationTriggers: screen-share could expose private data, credentials, customer info, or notifications
claim lacks proof or uses customer story/results
specialist asks for broad tools outside scope

customers.name: Tomorrow booked lead
customers.consent: meeting booked / no account access yet

extracted.services: First AI employee: missed lead capture, follow-up, or admin/onboarding helper
extracted.approvalBlockers: Need business details, first workflow, payment/commitment, approved channels/tools

customers.nextAgentAction: Prepare audit card and fill onboarding checklist during/after meeting. Do not send, connect accounts, or mutate customer systems without approval.

customers.name: Jarid / Paperclip
customers.nextAgentAction: Prepare customer setup checklist and access request packet; do not connect accounts or send messages.

customers.nextAgentAction: Convert meeting brief into implementation checklist and proof packet skeleton.

missing: Replace placeholder lead data with approved source rows
Attach real artifacts to approval cards
customers.nextAgentAction: Keep all automations local-only and surface approve/deny/defer cards before external action.

intakeQuestions: What is the customer paying for first: reply handling, missed-call recovery, content, onboarding, or ops cleanup?
Which document/source did this come from and can we keep a link to the original?
Which accounts/tools are explicitly approved to connect?
What must always come back to Colton before execution?
What evidence proves the setup worked?
documentAgentSteps: Upload or paste an approved local file/note placeholder
Extract customer, contact, website, services, tools, access needs, offer/pricing, blockers
Store original file name/source link beside the extracted record
Update the selected customer/lead record locally
Write a receipt with before/after, missing fields, and next safe action

extracted.services: Customer onboarding automation, document intake, approval-gated AI employees
extracted.currentTools: OpenClaw Mission Control, Business OS, receipts ledger
extracted.approvalBlockers: No external sends, account connects, or customer-visible changes without Colton approval

swipeDeck.title: Pick default offer for tomorrow lead
swipeDeck.body: Starter $1k+$500/mo, Standard $1.5k+$1k/mo, or Custom $2.5k+$1.5k+. Recommendation: Standard unless scope is tiny.

swipeDeck.title: Use on-the-spot onboarding checklist
swipeDeck.body: Collect company, owner, website, lead sources, tools, first AI employee, access needed, approval rules, payment status, first-session win.
